core_mechanism: finite-rate quench through a continuous critical point producing power-law scaling of the emergent order parameter with the driving rate, set by the transition's universal critical exponents (Kibble-Zurek-type critical-slowing-down scaling)
brief_reason: The abstract studies the dynamics of a slow phase transition and derives analytic scaling properties of the order parameter (magnetization) as the system is driven through the critical point, verified numerically — the hallmark of finite-rate-quench power-law scaling near a continuous transition.
